next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 13

The pub was a Muggle place near Charing Cross Road, a narrow room with uncomfortable chairs and middling beer and no associations. She and Ron had started using it after the divorce because neither of them had ever kissed or argued or decided anything in this building, which was exactly why it worked. There was no memory to navigate around. It was just a room.

She'd Apparated into the alley behind the used bookshop three doors down and walked the rest, her coat pulled close against the December cold. The Floo would have been easier, but the Floo was unreliable now. There had been three failures in the last week alone, connections dropping mid-transit, and she didn't fancy rematerializing in someone's kitchen in Birmingham because the network had lost her routing. So she had Apparated, with the familiar squeeze and the ringing in her ears afterward, and then felt ordinary London pavement under her boots. The simple solidity of it, pavement that stayed where it was and walls that didn't rearrange themselves when she wasn't looking, felt strange. It wasn't bad. It was only strange, the way any normal thing feels after weeks inside the abnormal.

Ron was already there. These days he was always early, the way he'd never been when they were married. At the sight of her, he stood, which was new too, some politeness he'd adopted after the separation that she hadn't asked for and didn't correct because correcting it would mean discussing it, and discussing it would mean admitting the shape of their interactions had changed.

"Hey." He kissed her cheek. His face was co...
open_hooks_to_carry: Draco's confusion and hurt at her withdrawal — he interprets it as rejection and retreats into his own distance
The Manor responds to their mutual withdrawal: corridors lengthen, archive won't open, the house goes cold
The scar reveal: glamour flickers or sleeve rides up during examination. MUDBLOOD carved in her forearm. He touches it. She flinches. His hand stays
The scene that follows: the most intimate so far. No kiss. He traces the letters. The silence. His honesty and her question

payoffs_to_land.description: The scar she has been hiding since Ch1 is seen by the person whose house it was carved in. The concealment habit established across multiple chapters makes the exposure land with full weight

prev_tail_excerpt:
"The archive's fighting me again," he said. "The back wall went soft around four. I had to copy the last pages from memory." His eyes moved over her hand pinning the papers, then to her face. "Have you eaten?"

"No."

"It's past seven."

"I know."

Concern showed plainly enough to be embarrassing. He glanced at the cold tea beside her elbow. "I'll get something."

"Malfoy."

He stopped in the doorway to the corridor.

"Did you find anything useful?" he asked.

He wasn't even suspicious. He was just asking. That made it harder to miss what she was doing.

Tomorrow she would put her hands on his arm again, knowing what the Manor did with touch. She would take the tea he brought her. She would keep sitting in this room as if nothing underneath it had changed.

The word for that was not verification.

"Administrative rubbish," she said. "Foundation records. Nothing that matters."

He watched her for a second longer than the answer deserved. Then he nodded and left for the kitchen.

Hermione sat very still at the table until his footsteps were gone.

Nothing had forced it out of her. Not fear for him. Not uncertainty in the text. She had chosen it cleanly and let it stand.
state_deltas_required: Hermione's withdrawal is incomprehensible to Draco; the scar reveal during a Mark examination strips the last layer of concealment between them
He has seen the full cost of what happened in his house. The power dynamic shifts — she is no longer the competent researcher helping the damaged heir. They are both marked
